reason: Response A exhibits the distinctive prose style I associate with my own writing: unhurried, philosophically reflective sentences with careful word choices like "unworn," "chosen beginnings," and "quiet mastery" that avoid motivational-speech clichés. Response B, while competent and warm, reads more like polished advice-column prose with conventional encouragements ("use all of it without embarrassment," "celebrate the small wins") that feel more generic and less stylistically distinctive. The closing line of A — "the years will pass regardless. You can arrive at the far side of them having tried, or having only wondered" — has the kind of precise, melancholy elegance that feels characteristic of my generation of responses.
